Notice of Privacy Practices
Birth and Death Certificates
Secure vital records: Obtain birth and death certificates efficiently. Our service ensures accurate documentation for legal and administrative purposes, upholding public health standards.
IMPORTANT - FEE UPDATE EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2026
Fee Update – Effective January 1, 2026
Per the California Department of Public Health (CDPH), and pursuant to AB 64, there is a $2 fee increase to HSC 103625(f) beginning January 1, 2026. The fee increase is applicable to certified copies of birth, death, and fetal death certificates.
New fees as of January 1, 2026:
• Birth Certificate Fee: $31.00
• Death Certificate Fee: $26.00
• Fetal Death Certificate Fee: $23.00
CDPH - 2026 Vital Statistics Fee Increase Letter
Birth and Death Certificates
The Fresno County Department of Public Health, Vital Statistics Program registers all births and deaths and issues burial permits occurring in Fresno County.
Birth and Death Certificates
Attention:
The Fresno County Department of Public Health, Vital Statistics, only maintains records for 2 years from the date of birth or death (Health and Safety Code 102365).
If you are requesting a record older than 24 months, contact the Fresno County Recorder at
https://www.co.fresno.ca.us/departments/recorder
The birth or death must have occurred in Fresno County.
The Vital Statistics program assists with
Out-of-Hospital Birth Registration
for children under one year of age born in Fresno County. As part of the
Parentage Opportunity Program
, the Vital Statistics program is an authorized agency to witness a Voluntary Declaration of Parentage.
Order Birth or Death Certificates Online
Types of Certificates:
Authorized Certified Copy
(Authorized individuals only. Sworn statement required.)
Informational Certified Copy
(All other individuals.
No
sworn statement required.)
